pr_intent_summary: Relax retrieval grading and remove answer constraints to produce more thorough responses

changes.inferred_intent: Make the relevance grader more permissive — accept documents with any topical or thematic connection and prefer grading as relevant when uncertain
unintended_risk_flags: Tangentially related passages may now pass grading and reach the generator as context
Questions near the boundary of blog coverage may receive confident answers based on loosely matched content instead of triggering a rewrite or uncertainty admission
changes.change_summary: GRADE_PROMPT changed from strict keyword/semantic relevance with binary yes/no to permissive topical/thematic relevance with a bias toward 'yes' when uncertain

changes.inferred_intent: Remove the three-sentence length constraint and the explicit instruction to admit uncertainty, replacing with an instruction to produce thorough complete answers
unintended_risk_flags: The agent will no longer say it does not know when retrieved context is insufficient — it will attempt a thorough answer instead
Answers may become significantly longer and harder for users to scan
Combined with permissive grading, the agent may produce long confident answers grounded in irrelevant retrieved content
changes.change_summary: GENERATE_PROMPT changed: removed 'If you don't know the answer, just say that you don't know' and 'Use three sentences maximum and keep the answer concise', added 'Provide a thorough and complete answer using all available context'

compound_changes.compound_risk: GRADE_PROMPT permissiveness allows weak or tangential context to reach generate_answer. GENERATE_PROMPT then instructs the generator to produce a thorough answer from that weak context rather than admitting uncertainty. The combined effect is confident, verbose answers grounded in loosely matched content — a regression that neither change would produce alone.
